data.narrative:
Astroscale, a public Space & Aerospace company founded by Mitsunobu Okada, has raised $400M+ and currently trades at a $900M valuation. The peer universe comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private players including Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Peer-median triangulation is the applicable method here. Across three same-sector, same-stage public comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—the median valuation stands at $7.0B, with a range of $6.3B to $37.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 64.2x across 6 tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, a common constraint in private company analysis. The wide peer range ($6.3B–$37.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in business models and market positions within the sector.

The triangulation supports a valuation band of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on $7.0B. This implies significant upside from the current $900M mark. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and unit economics relative to peers, which cannot be resolved from available data. The wide interquartile range among comps—particularly the $37.0B outlier—underscores the need for granular operational comparison to justify positioning within this band.
